serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 320 ml can of Coca-Cola, equivalent to approximately 336 g, is the typical single-serve portion sold in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Coca-Cola (Carbonated Beverage) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Coca-Cola 320 ml; per-100 g figures preserved as supplied. USDA FoodData Central entry 174851 (Carbonated beverage, cola, regular) cross-checked for plausibility: energy 37 kcal, carbohydrate 9.56 g, sugar 9.56 g — consistent with HPB values within expected formulation variance.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, regular — Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load values
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for regular Coca-Cola is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). GL per serving calculated as: GI (63) × carbohydrate per serving (33.8 g for 336 g serving) / 100 = 21.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar per 100 g; AHA 2018 identifies SSBs as independently raising blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention and weight gain, irrespective of the beverage's low sodium content (3 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Coca-Cola and all sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages; replace with water, sparkling water, or unsweetened drinks to reduce SSB-mediated blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 9.94 g free sugar per 100 g (33.4 g per 320 ml can) with zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial blood glucose spikes and contributing to insulin resistance with habitual consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of Type 2 Diabetes must avoid regular Coca-Cola; the rapid glycaemic impact (GI 63, GL ~21 per can) and absence of fibre make it a high-risk SSB that should be replaced with water or zero-sugar alternatives discussed with a healthcare provider.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased risk of cardiovascular events via dyslipidaemia, weight gain, elevated triglycerides, and systemic inflammation; one 320 ml can delivers approximately 33.4 g of added sugar.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should eliminate regular Coca-Cola from their diet; habitual SSB intake is an independent cardiovascular risk factor and a single can exceeds the AHA daily added-sugar limit for high-risk individuals.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High free-sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with zero fibre and no anti-inflammatory components; refined sugar promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Coca-Cola; its high sugar load promotes systemic inflammation, and the phosphoric acid content may adversely affect bone density in patients on corticosteroids.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola (320 ml can) is a globally recognised ultra-processed sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drink produced by The Coca-Cola Company. It is sold in Singapore in a standard 320 ml aluminium can. The beverage is made from carbonated water, sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. It has a characteristic sweet, slightly acidic, and lightly bitter cola flavour with strong effervescence. Per 100 g it provides 42 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 10.36 g carbohydrate of which 9.94 g is sugar, with negligible sodium (3 mg) and zero dietary fibre. A single 320 ml can delivers approximately 141 kcal and 33.4 g of free sugar, exceeding the WHO daily free-sugar recommendation of 25 g for adults. The glycaemic index is 63 (medium) and the glycaemic load per can is approximately 21, making it a significant glycaemic challenge. Coca-Cola is classified as an ultra-processed food (NOVA Group 4) and is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) as defined by the WHO, AHA, and Singapore HPB. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. In Singapore it is widely consumed as a mealtime accompaniment or standalone refreshment across all ethnic communities.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
How much sugar is in a 320ml Coke?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of Coca-Cola?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database for Coca-Cola 320 ml, an exact product match. Values cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entry 174851 for regular cola and found consistent within expected formulation variance. GI value sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008).
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero — Coca-Cola contains no protein by product formulation; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central (entry 174851). fat_g is confirmed zero — Coca-Cola contains no fat by product formulation; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — carbonated sugar-water beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero — plant-derived beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. magnesium_mg reported as 0.0 per HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB Foodo for this product; USDA FoodData Central entry 174851 does not report these fields for branded cola. Serving size of 336 g used for GL calculation (320 ml × 1.05 g/ml density approximation). Cultural classification as 'Chinese' in the input record reflects the Chinese-language branding on the Singapore product; the beverage's true origin is American and this is reflected in region_of_origin. The input aliases array contained a structured object; this has been normalised to a flat string array per RESULT template requirements.
